projects.title: Tin Can Organizer
projects.description: A multi-compartment organizer made from a paint can and tin cans for storing supplies.
steps: Mark and punch holes in the sides of empty tin cans
Drill the punched holes to fit screws or bolts
Optionally paint or decorate the cans
Mark corresponding holes on the paint bucket and drill them
Attach the tin cans to the paint bucket with bolts, washers, and nuts
Use the assembled organizer to store pens, brushes, or tools
safety_notes: Wear gloves to avoid sharp metal edges
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses scrap metal cans to create a useful desktop organizer, reducing waste and saving money.

projects.title: Woven Bottle Vase
projects.description: A flower vase made by cutting and weaving strips of a recycled plastic bottle.
steps: Cut off the top of a plastic bottle about 3 inches from the rim
Measure and cut evenly spaced vertical strips in the remaining cylinder
Fold the strips outward to form the vase rim
Starting with one strip, weave it under and over the next strips
Continue weaving each strip until all are woven into the basket shape
Tuck the end strips into the weave to secure them
safety_notes: Be careful of sharp plastic edges when cutting
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Transforms a plastic bottle into a decorative vase, reducing plastic waste.

projects.title: Bottle Coin Purse
projects.description: A zippered coin purse made from two halves of a plastic soda bottle.
required_items: plastic bottles (2 of the same size)
steps: Cut off the bottoms of two identical plastic bottles
Remove any jagged edges with scissors
Punch holes around the top edge of one bottle half
Place a zipper between the two bottle halves and sew it on through the holes
Sew the other half of the zipper to the second bottle half
Zip up the purse and trim any excess zipper
safety_notes: Handle plastic edges and sewing needle carefully
Keep small parts away from children
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses plastic bottles to make a useful coin purse, preventing waste.

projects.title: Bottle Lava Lamp
projects.description: A kid-friendly lava lamp experiment using a plastic bottle, oil, water, and fizzing tablets.
steps: Wash and dry an empty plastic bottle
Fill the bottle two-thirds full with vegetable oil
Fill the rest of the bottle with water, leaving a little air at the top
Add a few drops of food coloring and let it settle
Drop pieces of Alka-Seltzer or similar effervescent tablet into the bottle
Watch colorful bubbles rise and fall like a lava lamp
safety_notes: Use child-safe materials and supervise children
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Converts a plastic bottle into an educational and fun lamp-like decoration, reusing waste material.

projects.title: Mason Jar Herb Garden
projects.description: A windowsill herb garden using upcycled mason jars as planters.
steps: Clean jars and add a layer of pebbles for drainage
Add a layer of perlite on top of the pebbles
Fill partially with potting soil and plant an herb seedling or seeds
Cover with more soil, pat down gently, and water lightly
Place jars in a sunny spot and water when soil is dry
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses glass jars to grow herbs indoors, cutting down on plastic pot use and waste.

projects.title: Tin Can Lantern
projects.description: A decorative lantern made by punching a pattern of holes into a recycled tin can.
steps: Remove the can label and clean the can
Tape or draw a pattern on the can surface
Fill the can with water and freeze it overnight
Use a hammer and nail to punch holes along the pattern in the frozen can
Let ice melt, dry the can, and optionally paint it
Attach a wire handle and place a candle inside for ambient light
safety_notes: Wear gloves to handle sharp edges
Be careful with hammering and use eye protection
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Turns a metal can into a decorative lantern, keeping it out of the landfill and creating ambiance.

projects.title: Egg Carton Seed Starter
projects.description: Use recycled cardboard egg carton cups as biodegradable seed starters for gardening.
steps: Cut the egg carton into individual cups
Poke small drainage holes in the bottom of each cup
Fill each cup with potting soil
Plant seeds in the soil and cover lightly
Water gently and place cartons in a tray until seedlings sprout
Once seedlings are large enough, plant the entire cup into the ground
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses compostable cardboard cartons to start plants, reducing waste and aiding plant growth.

projects.title: Cardboard Tube Seed Starter
projects.description: Biodegradable seed pots made by cutting toilet paper rolls in half and filling with soil.
steps: Cut toilet paper rolls in half (optional: use paper towel rolls cut into thirds)
Poke drainage holes in the bottom of each tube
Fill each tube with potting soil
Plant seeds and cover lightly with soil
Keep tubes moist and place in sunlight until seedlings sprout
Plant tubes directly into ground when seedlings outgrow the cups
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Turns cardboard waste into plantable seedling pots, cutting down on plastic trays.

projects.title: Cardboard Tube Binoculars
projects.description: Kids craft binoculars made by gluing two cardboard tubes together and adding a strap.
steps: Glue two toilet paper tubes side by side
Wrap the tubes with paper or decorate as desired
Use a pen to poke holes on the outer sides of each tube
Thread a string through the holes and knot on each side to make a strap
Let glue dry before giving to a child to play with
safety_notes: Keep strap short to avoid choking hazard
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses cardboard tubes to create a fun toy, encouraging imaginative play with waste material.

projects.title: Jute Rope Basket
projects.description: A storage basket made by wrapping a recycled box in jute rope.
steps: Tape one end of the jute rope to the box
Wrap the rope around the box, gluing periodically to secure
Continue wrapping until the box is completely covered
Cut and glue the final end of the rope under the box
Optionally, place a fabric liner inside the basket
safety_notes: Wash rope in case of dust
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses scrap rope and cardboard to make a decorative basket, giving new life to an old box.

projects.title: Wine Cork Coasters
projects.description: Hexagonal coasters made by hot-gluing sliced wine corks together.
steps: Slice wine corks evenly with a sharp knife
Arrange the slices in a honeycomb pattern
Glue the cork slices side by side to form a coaster
Optionally glue fabric or webbing on the bottom for grip
Trim any uneven edges and let glue dry
safety_notes: Hot glue can burn, use carefully
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses cork stoppers to protect surfaces, preventing them from being thrown away.

projects.title: Coconut Shell Planter
projects.description: A rustic hanging planter made from a recycled coconut shell filled with soil and succulents.
steps: Carefully split open the coconut and remove all coconut meat
Clean and dry the inside of the shell
Fill the shell with potting soil and plant a succulent or small plant
Cut jute rope into four equal lengths and tie them together
Hang the planter by the jute rope above a light area
safety_notes: Use sharp tools carefully to open coconut
Support coconut while drilling holes for hanging
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Turns a natural waste product into a cute planter, combining reuse with gardening.

projects.title: T-Shirt Tote Bag
projects.description: A reusable tote bag made by no-sew upcycling of an old T-shirt.
steps: Cut off the sleeves from the T-shirt
Cut out the neckline to create bag straps
Turn the shirt inside out and cut slits from the bottom up to the marked bottom line
Tie each pair of front and back slits together to seal the bottom
Turn the shirt right-side out; the bottom is now a solid base
safety_notes: Tie knots tightly to prevent unravelling
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Converts old clothing into a sturdy bag, keeping fabric out of landfills.

projects.title: Shoebox Cable Organizer
projects.description: A custom cable storage box made by inserting cardboard tube pieces in a shoebox.
steps: Measure the height of the shoebox and cut cardboard rolls to match
Arrange and tape rolls inside the box to create compartments
Cover all empty space with tubes to fit snugly
Label each tube position if desired
Wind each cable and place into its own tube compartment
safety_notes: Ensure cables are unplugged before organizing
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses leftover cardboard and an old shoebox to prevent cord tangles and promote reuse.

projects.title: Self-Watering Planter
projects.description: An indoor planter made by inverting a cut plastic bottle to self-water plants.
steps: Cut a plastic bottle in half
Invert the top half into the bottom half
Place a piece of sponge or cloth in the bottle neck as a wick
Fill the top with potting soil and plant seeds or seedlings
Fill the bottom reservoir with water to provide self-watering
safety_notes: Keep top dry to avoid mold
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses plastic bottles to create a simple watering system for plants.

projects.title: T-Shirt Dog Tug Toy
projects.description: A braided tug toy for dogs made from strips of an old T-shirt.
steps: Cut the T-shirt into three long strips
Tie the strips together with an overhand knot at one end
Braid the three strips tightly
Tie a final knot at the other end
Trim excess fabric from knots if needed
safety_notes: Do not leave pet unattended with toy
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses old clothing to create a durable pet toy, reducing waste.

projects.title: Cookie Mix in a Jar
projects.description: Layer baking mix ingredients in a jar to gift or bake homemade cookies.
steps: Clean and dry a wide-mouth mason jar
Measure and layer dry ingredients (flour, sugar, etc.) into the jar
Fill jar close to top, leaving minimal air
Seal jar and attach a recipe card for the mix
Decorate the jar with ribbon if desired
safety_notes: Ensure jar lid is secure to prevent spills
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses a glass jar to give a sustainable homemade gift, reducing packaging waste.

projects.title: Pampering Kit in a Jar
projects.description: A spa or beauty gift set assembled inside a recycled mason jar.
steps: Collect travel-size spa or beauty items (e.g., lotions, bath bombs)
Place items inside a clean mason jar
Add cotton balls or other fillers to keep items in place
Decorate jar with ribbon or label
Add gift tag if desired
safety_notes: Check for allergies if giving lotions or creams
Jar lid should be tightly closed
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses a recycled jar to package eco-friendly gift items, reducing waste from new packaging.

projects.title: Tin Can Pen Holder
projects.description: A simple desk pen or pencil holder made by decorating an upcycled tin can.
steps: Remove the label and clean the tin can
Decorate or paint the exterior if desired
Place pens and pencils inside for storage
Optionally add a base to the can for stability
safety_notes: Ensure can is clean of food residue
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses a food can to organize office supplies, reducing waste and providing function.

projects.title: Tin Can Planter
projects.description: A decorative plant pot made by painting and filling a recycled tin can with soil.
steps: Clean and dry an empty tin can
Paint the outside for color (optional)
Punch drainage holes in the bottom
Fill with potting soil and plant a small flower or herb
Place in a sunny spot
safety_notes: Use appropriate soil for plant type
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Transform a metal can into a useful planter, reducing waste and adding greenery.

projects.title: Mason Jar Candle Holder
projects.description: A decorative lantern or candle holder made by placing a tea light in a cleaned glass jar.
steps: Clean and dry a glass jar
Insert a tea light or LED candle inside
Tie twine or ribbon around the jar top for decoration (optional)
Place on table or hang using wire handle
safety_notes: Do not leave lit candles unattended
Use LED tea lights for safety
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses a jar to safely hold a candle, repurposing glass and adding cozy lighting.

projects.title: Plastic Bottle Bird Feeder
projects.description: A simple bird feeder made by cutting a plastic bottle and adding perches for birds to eat seeds.
steps: Clean a plastic bottle and remove label
Cut small windows near the bottom sides
Insert wooden spoons or sticks through the bottle to serve as perches
Fill the bottle with bird seed
Hang the bottle feeder from a tree
safety_notes: Check birds are safe using feeder
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses a plastic bottle to feed wildlife, preventing it from entering the waste stream.

projects.title: Plastic Bottle Self-Watering Planter
projects.description: A self-watering planter for indoor plants made from a plastic bottle and a wick.
steps: Cut the top off a plastic bottle
Invert the top half inside the bottom half
Tie a cotton wick through the bottle neck
Fill upper part with soil and plant
Fill lower part with water, allowing wick to draw water
safety_notes: Keep water level filled to sustain plants
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses plastic bottles to create an automated watering system for houseplants.

projects.title: Wood Scrap Coasters
projects.description: Coasters made by cutting and gluing small pieces of scrap wood together.
steps: Cut wood scraps into uniform small blocks
Arrange blocks to form a coaster shape
Glue blocks together and let dry
Sand the surface if needed and finish with varnish
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses leftover lumber pieces to make functional drink coasters, reducing wood waste.

projects.title: Woven Newspaper Basket
projects.description: A small basket woven from rolled-up strips of old newspaper.
steps: Roll newspaper pages tightly into long tubes
Arrange a square or circle base and start weaving tubes over and under
Continue weaving around, adding tubes as needed to increase size
Glue end tubes securely and shape the basket walls
safety_notes: Finished basket may be fragile, use for lightweight items
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Turns waste newspaper into a useful container, lowering paper waste.

projects.title: Magazine Paper Beads
projects.description: Beads made by rolling strips of colorful magazine pages, then sealing them with glue.
steps: Cut magazines into long triangular strips
Roll each strip tightly around a toothpick
Glue the end and seal each bead with varnish
String beads to make jewelry or hangings
safety_notes: Ensure glue is dried before use
Use small beads cautiously around children
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses colorful magazine pages to create decorative beads, diverting paper from trash.

projects.title: Popsicle Stick Photo Frame
projects.description: A picture frame made by gluing popsicle sticks together, then painting or decorating it.
steps: Arrange four sticks in a square and glue corners together
Glue additional sticks on top for thickness and stability
Decorate frame with paint or markers
Attach photo to the back or use clips to hold it
safety_notes: Ensure frame is completely dry before use
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses craft sticks to make a personal frame, encouraging reuse of simple materials.

projects.title: Branch Coat Rack
projects.description: A rustic coat rack made by attaching a sturdy tree branch to a wall or frame.
steps: Find a straight sturdy branch and cut to desired length
Sand the bark if necessary to smooth sharp spots
Drill holes and use screws to mount the branch to a wall or wooden board
Add coat hooks along the branch if needed
Hang coats or hats on the branch knobs
safety_notes: Do not hang heavy items beyond branch strength
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses natural branch to create unique storage, reducing waste and adding charm.

projects.title: Tire Garden Planter
projects.description: A colorful garden planter made by painting and filling an old tire with soil.
steps: Clean the tire and paint it if desired
Cut a section off to lower the edges (optional)
Place the tire in a garden spot
Fill with potting soil and plant flowers or vegetables
safety_notes: Ensure tire is stable and filled solidly
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Repurposes a large durable object into a planter, keeping tires out of landfills.

projects.title: Fabric Scrap Quilt
projects.description: A small patchwork quilt made from leftover fabric scraps sewn together.
tools_required: sewing machine or needle and thread
steps: Cut fabric scraps into uniform squares or shapes
Sew pieces together to form a quilt top
Layer with batting and backing fabric
Sew layers together by hand or machine
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Combines textile remnants into a cozy quilt, reducing fabric waste.

projects.title: Woven Magazine Basket
projects.description: A small basket woven from strips cut from old magazines.
steps: Cut magazines into long strips
Roll strips into paper tubes
Weave tubes into a basket shape
Secure the ends with glue and trim excess
safety_notes: Finished basket is decorative, not for heavy use
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses glossy paper to create storage, keeping magazines out of recycling bin.

projects.title: Popsicle Stick Mini Stool
projects.description: A small stool or stand built by gluing together layers of popsicle sticks.
steps: Glue sticks side by side to form a solid square base
Stack and glue additional layers on top
Allow glue to dry between layers
Sand rough edges and paint if desired
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Builds furniture from craft sticks, providing a small platform without new materials.

projects.title: Rubber Band Bracelets
projects.description: Colorful bracelets woven from recycled rubber bands, using a simple loom or hook.
steps: Gather colorful rubber bands
Loop bands through each other in a chain, using fingers or a loom
Secure the end with a knot or small hook
Wear as a bracelet or give as a gift
safety_notes: Not suitable for young children unsupervised
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses leftover rubber bands to make jewelry, repurposing elastic waste.

projects.title: Paper Bag Gift Box
projects.description: A decorative gift box made from folding and decorating a brown paper bag.
steps: Cut off the handles off a paper bag
Fold the open top down to desired height
Press sides to form box corners
Decorate with ribbon or stickers
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Transforms a disposable bag into a gift box, cutting down on waste wrapping paper.

projects.title: Cardboard Laptop Stand
projects.description: A simple laptop stand made by cutting and folding a sturdy piece of cardboard.
steps: Measure and cut cardboard to laptop size
Fold edges to create a sloped platform
Reinforce bends with extra cardboard strips
Paint or decorate if desired
safety_notes: Ensure stand is stable before use
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses cardboard to elevate a laptop, promoting a better setup without new materials.

projects.title: Clothespin Table Lamp
projects.description: A table lamp with a base made by gluing wood clothespins together.
steps: Glue clothespins side by side to form a cylinder
Continue gluing layers until desired height is reached
Attach lamp hardware on top
Place a bulb and lampshade
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Creates a stylish lamp base from clothespins, reusing surplus clothespins creatively.

projects.title: Shoebox Vending Machine Toy
projects.description: A pretend-play vending machine made by decorating a shoebox.
steps: Decorate the shoebox to look like a vending machine
Cut a coin slot and product window
Glue in a flap or chutes
Use for pretend play with toy coins
safety_notes: Keep small parts away from children under 3
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses a shoebox to create a fun toy, encouraging imagination instead of waste.

projects.title: Cardboard Spool Holder
projects.description: An organizer for ribbon or thread using a toilet paper roll inside a small box.
steps: Tape the toilet roll inside the box
Thread ribbon or thread through roll
Pull ends out of the box and close lid
safety_notes: Ensure ends are free to prevent tangling
Use box size that fits roll
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Keeps thread or ribbon neat using waste materials, reducing clutter.

projects.title: Tin Can Desk Caddy
projects.description: A desk caddy for pens and notes made by gluing multiple soup cans together.
steps: Clean and dry tin cans
Arrange cans in desired formation and glue sides together
Decorate with paper or paint
Use compartments to store pens, paperclips, etc.
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Transforms cans into a functional desktop organizer, saving money and waste.

projects.title: Glass Jar Spice Rack
projects.description: A wall-mounted spice rack made by attaching empty glass jars to a wooden board.
steps: Mount a wooden board on the wall
Glue or screw lids to board in rows
Fill jars with spices and screw them onto lids
Label jars on top for identification
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses jars to organize spices, reducing plastic use and clutter.

projects.title: Plastic Container Seedling Tray
projects.description: Seedlings started in a recycled plastic food container with drainage holes.
steps: Clean the container and drill holes in bottom
Fill with potting soil
Plant seeds or seedlings
Place in sunlight and water regularly
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Repurposes plastic containers to start plants, delaying disposal and saving money.

projects.title: Glass Jar Pencil Holder
projects.description: A pencil or utensil holder made from a painted or decorated glass jar.
steps: Clean and dry the jar
Paint or wrap twine around it for decoration
Place pens, pencils or utensils inside
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses a jar to organize desk tools, giving glass a second life.

projects.title: Denim Storage Bin
projects.description: A storage bin or bag made by sewing old denim jeans into a fabric container.
steps: Cut off legs from old jeans
Sew the open ends of the legs closed to create a bag shape
Use the waistband for handles (optional)
Decorate or reinforce as needed
Use it to store toys or laundry
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses durable denim fabric to make a practical storage container, keeping clothes out of the trash.

projects.title: Denim Pocket Organizer
projects.description: A wall-hanging organizer made from the pocket portion of old jeans.
steps: Cut pocket sections off old jeans
Attach pockets to a wooden board or directly to wall with nails
Hang board or mount pockets on wall
Use pockets to hold small items like remotes, mail, or tools
safety_notes: Ensure organizer is secure on wall
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Gives extra life to jeans by turning pockets into storage pouches.

projects.title: Scrap Fabric Coasters
projects.description: Coasters made by sewing together small pieces of leftover fabric scraps.
tools_required: needle and thread or sewing machine
steps: Cut fabric scraps into squares or circles
Layer fabric pieces (and batting) and sew around edges
Combine different fabrics for a patchwork look
Press flat and use to protect surfaces
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Uses leftover cloth to make useful coasters, reducing textile waste.

projects.title: Rope Plant Hanger
projects.description: A hanging planter made from jute rope or macrame, created without buying new materials.
steps: Cut rope into equal lengths
Knot or weave the ropes together in macrame style
Make a cradle or basket to hold a pot
Hang the ropes from ceiling or beam to suspend the plant
safety_notes: Ensure knots are tight to hold weight
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Upcycles rope to display plants stylishly, avoiding plastic planters.

projects.title: Bottle Cap Magnets
projects.description: Fridge magnets made by decorating bottle caps and adding a magnet on the back.
steps: Clean and decorate bottle caps (paint or photos)
Glue a small magnet inside each cap
Attach to fridge or magnetic board
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Transforms cap litter into cute magnets, giving them new purpose.

projects.title: Cardboard Phone Stand
projects.description: A simple stand for a phone made by cutting and folding a sturdy piece of cardboard.
steps: Cut a piece of cardboard into a stand shape with a slot
Fold along lines to create an angled back support
Place phone in the stand slot
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Repurposes cardboard to make a handy phone stand instead of buying plastic.

projects.title: Newspaper Wreath
projects.description: A decorative wreath made from rolled or folded pages of newspaper.
steps: Roll newspaper pages into cones or tubes
Form a circular base and glue cones around it overlapping
Add bows or decorations as desired
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Turns old newspapers into a festive decoration, keeping paper from landfill.

projects.title: Fabric Scrap Basket
projects.description: A small storage basket made by twisting and coiling strips of leftover fabric.
steps: Cut fabric into long strips and twist each into a rope
Coil the rope into a spiral, stitching layers together
Continue until basket reaches desired size
Tuck and sew the end under to finish
projects.reason_for_upcycling: Reuses textile scraps to make an attractive and functional storage basket.
